channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: The U.S. will resume Ukraine military aid and intelligence, as Kyiv approves ceasefire
item.description: The United States said it will resume sending military aid and intelligence to Ukraine, as Ukraine agreed to a Trump administration proposal for a monthlong ceasefire.
item.dc:creator: Joanna Kakissis, Michele Kelemen, and Eleanor Beardsley, MPR News

item.title: A government program made tax filing free and more efficient. Musk and DOGE may get rid of it anyway
item.description: The fate of the IRS Direct File program remains unclear as Elon Musk and the Department of Government Efficiency cleave their way through the federal bureaucracy.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Rubio could face an unfriendly reception from close G7 allies over Trump's policies
item.description: LA MALBAIE, Canada (AP) — U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio may be walking into unusually unfriendly territory this week when he meets his counterparts from the Group of 7 industrialized democracies — strong American allies stunned by President Donald Trump’s actions against them.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Idaho becomes first state to prefer death by firing squad for executions
item.description: Idaho Gov. Brad Little signed a bill on Wednesday making death by firing squad the preferred method for executions, and death by lethal injection the second preferred method.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Illinois Dem lawmaker pushes bill to legalize attacks on police for people having mental health episode
item.description: An Illinois lawmaker has introduced a bill that critics say will make it legal for anyone experiencing a mental health episode to attack police officers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump campaigned as a protector of free speech. Critics say his actions as president threaten it
item.description: DENVER (AP) — When President Donald Trump gave his joint address to Congress last week, he boasted that in his first few weeks back in the White House he had “brought free speech back to America.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI and CHRISTINE FERNANDO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kuwait frees a group of jailed Americans, including contractors held on drug charges
item.description: Kuwait has released a group of American prisoners, including veterans and military contractors jailed for years on drug-related charges.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker And Jon Gambrell, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Military leaders warn of risks to armed forces' readiness in temporary funding bill
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Military service leaders told senators Wednesday that passing a temporary budget that keeps defense spending largely flat will hurt readiness and efforts to modernize the armed forces.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge temporarily blocks parts of Trump order targeting Perkins Coie
item.description: A federal judge on Wednesday temporarily froze parts of President Trump’s executive order targeting the law firm Perkins Coie, which claims its past work for Democrats made it a subject of retaliation by the administration. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Irish PM asked why he 'let' Rosie O'Donnell move to his country
item.description: "Why in the world would you let Rosie O'Donnell move to Ireland?" asked Brain Glenn with the conservative digital media company Real America’s Voice.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: Judge dismisses lawsuit claiming tribal casinos in Minnesota are running illegal games
item.description: A federal district court judge has dismissed a lawsuit brought by one of Minnesota’s horse racing tracks against executives who oversee five tribal casinos in the state.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Olson, MPR News

item.title: US judge temporarily halts Trump plan to cut hundreds of millions of dollars for teacher training
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A federal judge in Boston on Tuesday blocked the Trump administration's plan to cut hundreds of millions of dollars for teacher training, finding that cuts are already affecting training programs aimed at addressing a nationwide teacher shortage. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Transition period to curb government hiring driven inflation: Stephen Miller
item.description: White House Deputy Chief of Staff for Policy and Homeland Security Adviser Stephen Miller joins NewsNation to discuss the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: House GOP tees up final vote on bill to avert shutdown
item.description: House Republicans on Tuesday advanced their bill to avert an end-of-the-week government shutdown, teeing up a final vote on the legislation later in the day. The chamber voted 216-214 to adopt a rule — which governs debate on legislation — for Speaker Mike Johnson’s (R-La.) continuing resolution.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Vance gives House GOP closing pitch on Trump-backed funding bill ahead of vote
item.description: Vice President Vance delivered a closing pitch in favor of Speaker Mike Johnson’s (R-La.) government funding bill during the House GOP’s private conference meeting Tuesday, hours before the lower chamber is scheduled to vote on the measure.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration halts funding for two cybersecurity efforts, including one for elections
item.description: The Trump administration has cut millions of dollars in federal funding from two cybersecurity initiatives, including one dedicated to helping state and local election officials.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump's approval dips amid concerns over economy: Poll
item.description: President Donald Trump’s approval rating has dropped a few points since he first took office, with voters increasingly expressing concerns over the state of the economy, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, Fox 5

item.title: Trump says he’s buying ‘brand new’ Tesla to support Musk
item.description: President Donald Trump said he would be buying a “brand new” Tesla to support his senior adviser Elon Musk, as the stock market shows shares of the electric car company tumbling.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: House subcommittee hears testimony on school choice
item.description: Some say parents should be able to choose how their children are educated, while others worry redirecting funds could gut public education.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: How reliable is the government’s economic data? Under Trump, there are real concerns
item.description: The disbanding of committees that consulted on government data — and comments from a senior official about changing how GDP is calculated — are raising alarm about the reliability of government data.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley, MPR News

item.title: Florida seeks easier voting access for hurricane-displaced citizens
item.description: Last year's hurricanes displaced thousands of Floridians, turning their lives upside down and making it even harder for some to vote during a crucial election year. Now, a Pinellas County state lawmaker wants to make it easier for people to cast their ballots after a storm.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ontario slaps 25% increase on electricity exports to U.S. in response to Trump's trade war
item.description: Trump launched a new trade war last week by imposing tariffs against Washington’s three biggest trading partners, drawing immediate retaliation from Mexico, Canada and China and sending financial markets into a tailspin.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: What to know about Mark Carney, elected next Canadian prime minister
item.description: Mark Carney, a former central banker, won his Liberal Party's election in a landslide on Sunday to replace Justin Trudeau as prime minister of Canada. He will be sworn into office in the coming days.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5
